signals.headline: Negotiations resuming:** US President Donald Trump confirmed that a new round of talks with Iran could begin within days
signals.raw: Negotiations resuming:** US President Donald Trump confirmed that a new round of talks with Iran could begin within days, with Pakistan offering to host negotiations again after last weekend's discussions in Islamabad failed to produce a breakthrough[1][2].

signals.headline: Ceasefire status:** US Vice President JD Vance confirmed the current ceasefire is holding, describing it as approximatel
signals.raw: Ceasefire status:** US Vice President JD Vance confirmed the current ceasefire is holding, describing it as approximately six to seven days old[1]. However, tensions remain fragile with the US imposing a maritime blockade on Iranian ports[2][4].

signals.headline: Market-moving takeaway:** No substantive breakthrough has been confirmed—only renewed diplomatic talks scheduled within
signals.raw: Market-moving takeaway:** No substantive breakthrough has been confirmed—only renewed diplomatic talks scheduled within days. The ceasefire remains in place but depends on upcoming negotiations succeeding where the Islamabad round failed.

signals.headline: Solana Alpenglow Upgrade Announced | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Alpenglow Upgrade Announced | YES | MED confidence**

The Alpenglow consensus upgrade aims to slash transaction finality from 12 seconds to 150 milliseconds, representing a significant performance improvement[3]. Solana has also hit 167 million monthly token holders with 148 million daily non-vote transactions[3].

signals.headline: Stalled progress:** Tehran attributes the breakdown to excessive US demands and lack of political will, while Washington
signals.raw: Stalled progress:** Tehran attributes the breakdown to excessive US demands and lack of political will, while Washington maintains Iran's nuclear demands are the core obstacle[1][4]. Trump declared the "end of Iran conflict" to Fox News host Maria Bartiromo, though this appears premature given the lack of formal agreement[3].

signals.headline: US demands:** The Trump administration is seeking a broader agreement that includes limits on Iran's nuclear ambitions i
signals.raw: US demands:** The Trump administration is seeking a broader agreement that includes limits on Iran's nuclear ambitions in exchange for economic normalization, with Trump emphasizing Iran cannot retain its enriched uranium stockpile[1][4].

signals.headline: Bitcoin Breaks $75,000 Amid Middle East Peace Talks | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Breaks $75,000 Amid Middle East Peace Talks | YES | HIGH confidence**

Bitcoin surged above $75,000 for the first time since mid-March after Iran signaled it wants to resume peace talks with the United States[4]. This geopolitical development has lifted the entire crypto market, with Ethereum rallying 8.6% to $2,377 and Solana gaining 6.3%[4].

signals.headline: Ethereum Institutional Inflows Resume | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Institutional Inflows Resume | YES | HIGH confidence**

Ethereum funds saw $196.5 million in inflows on April 14, ending three consecutive weeks of outflows[7]. This reversal signals renewed institutional confidence, particularly as on-chain activity jumped 41%, suggesting the rally has fundamental backing beyond short squeezes[4].

signals.headline: Solana Reclaims Stablecoin Volume Lead Over Ethereum | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana Reclaims Stablecoin Volume Lead Over Ethereum | YES | HIGH confidence**

Solana has overtaken Ethereum in adjusted stablecoin volume for the second consecutive week, capturing 32.6% versus Ethereum's 27.8%[2]. signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 Annual Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's March 2026 Annual Inflation Rises to 2.2% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in January and February, confirming data released around early April 2026 after the month's end; this beat the prior two months but fell short of market forecasts of 2.4%.[1]
